answer:
lean take it — but only if u actually want vc-scale outcome

quick math:
- $2m @ $12m post = ~16.7% dilution, thats cheap for seed. clean deal from solid fund = good signal
- $30k mrr @ 8%/mo → ~$76k mrr in 12mo, ~$69k arr→$900k arr ish. decent but not breakout
- default-alive means ur negotiating from strength — best time to raise. if growth slips to 4-5% & runway hits 6mo, terms get way worse or door closes

take it if:
- 8% growth is capital-constrained (cant hire, cant spend on gtm) — $2m buys ~2-3 eng/gtm hires + 24-30mo runway
- u want $100m+ outcome & r ok w/ board seat, pref stack, pressure 2 raise series A in ~18-24mo

keep bootstrapping if:
- 8% is demand-constrained (more $ wont speed it up) — then ur just selling 17% for nothing
- ur happy w/ path to $2-5m arr profitable co u own 100% of. thats life-changing $ w/o dilution
- vc treadmill locks u out of small exits: $12m post means <$36m exit = disappointing 4 fund, possible friction

middle path: try negotiating $2m @ $14-15m post or take $1-1.5m to cut dilution. also check terms: 1x non-participating pref only, no weird ratchets, board comp 2 founders + 1 investor max

my read: default-alive + solid fund + fair price = take it unless u genuinely dont know what ud spend it on. money u dont need is exactly the money 2 take — just make sure ur signing up 4 the venture path on purpose
